changes: Scan log window: after every scan a copyable log shows problems first (unreadable files, unsafe file names — with full paths), then files not found on disk (renamed/moved/deleted) with a one-click option to remove their database entries or keep them.

Leftover entries of renamed/deleted files no longer appear as work to do: the change-type tree, counts and severity dots skip them, and opening such an album explains the situation instead of showing an empty editor with '0 tracks'.

Stable problem-type order: the change-type tree is sorted by severity and a fixed workflow priority (ID3v1 rescue → encoding → text repairs → field fixes → images) — the order no longer shuffles after applying changes.
Track view: huge unbreakable custom-tag values (e.g. AcoustID fingerprints) are shortened for display — they can no longer stretch the window to absurd widths.
Titles containing < > characters display correctly in the headers.

ID3v1/ID3v2 conflict offers keep their current/proposed values: other rules no longer mistake the offered old-v1 value for the real tag (e.g. 'Kiioto' vs 'Kiiōtō' no longer spawns a bogus artist proposal that displaced the conflict offer).
Clear buttons: remove a field's existing value — per album (fields table) or per track (track view). Clearing proposes 'current value → nothing'; applying it deletes the tag from the MP3.
Clear and typed values replace each other: after Clear you can still type a replacement value (and vice versa) before applying.
Removal proposals are now clearly shown as '‹remove value›' in all change lists.
Emptying a proposed-value cell now consistently withdraws the proposal (in the album boxes, the album change list and the track view).
Auto-update: the app checks GitHub for new versions at startup (can be turned off) and offers to install them.
New 'Updates' tab in Settings: installed version, manual update check, what's new in each version, one-click update.
Updating downloads the new version, installs it and restarts the app; your settings, libraries and databases are never touched.
Initial release: library scanning, rule engine, review-and-apply workflow, tag history with revert, multi-condition search, online metadata on demand, themes and translatable field labels.
